title: If someone handed you $16 million today, what would honestly change?
author: Ok-Basil2753
selftext:
Although I have been retired for nearly a year, my investment strategy continues to evolve alongside market changes. The biggest shift hasn't been in my lifestyle, but in my mindset. I no longer chase every market surge or headline; instead, I view investing as a form of risk management rather than a source of entertainment.

Most of my profits stem from seemingly mundane practices:

1. Holding onto profitable positions far longer than I was initially comfortable with.

2. Keeping cash on hand while others panic sell.

3. Filtering out 90% of market noise.

4. Steadily building positions rather than trying to hit a "home run" (chasing massive, quick gains) every week.

Ironically, as my account size has grown, my trading frequency has actually decreased.

I share this not to offer investment advice, but because I wish someone had told me years ago that the compounding effect of steady, consistent accumulation far outweighs the gains from chasing the thrill of the trade.

I have consistently adhered to a relatively conservative strategy, which has enabled me to generate steady profits. The core philosophy is simple: I carefully select high quality individual stocks or ETFs by combining position sizing, diversification, trend following, and fundamental analysis. This approach allows me to effectively navigate extreme market volatility while precisely capturing growth opportunities.

Over the years, I have come to realize that knowledge grows faster when shared. The best investment opportunities I’ve found often emerged from discussions, research, and exchanges with people who hold different viewpoints. If my strategy resonates with you, please feel free to leave a comment or contact me so we can discuss and explore these ideas together.
